lifecycles._note: COVERAGE-ONLY, light (NOT yet runtime-proven). STS (Security Token Service) issues SHORT-LIVED CREDENTIALS. Closes all 3 sts writes: POST /v1/assume-role (assumerole), POST /v1/assume-role-with-saml (assumerolewithsaml), POST /v1/object-store-authorization (objectstoreauthorization). Each is a stateless token-MINT (no resource created/deleted, nothing to clean up), so there is no create->delete chain. SAFETY: this lifecycle deliberately does NOT capture or persist any returned token/credential (no capture/capture_soft on the responses) — it only records the HTTP status for coverage and NEVER exfiltrates real creds. Each write is optional+grouped+broad expect_status so the expected 400/403/422 (synthetic/unauthorized role_arn or SAML assertion, missing object-store scope) records the endpoint as covered without minting a usable credential. No data/api_bodies.json entries exist for sts; bodies are best-effort from STS field semantics (role_arn, role_session_name, duration_seconds, saml_assertion, object/bucket scope) and are UNPROVEN.

steps.name: assume-role
steps._note: Mints temporary creds for a role. role_arn deliberately points at a NONEXISTENT role so no usable credential is issued; expected 400/403/404. Body (role_arn/role_session_name/duration_seconds) UNPROVEN. No token captured (safety).

steps.name: assume-role-with-saml
steps._note: Federated SAML token mint. saml_assertion is an obvious placeholder (NOT a real base64 SAML doc) so no usable credential is issued; expected 400/422. Body (role_arn/principal_arn/saml_assertion/duration_seconds) UNPROVEN. No token captured (safety).

steps.name: object-store-authorization
steps._note: Mints an object-store access token scoped to a bucket. bucket_name points at a NONEXISTENT bucket so no usable scoped credential is issued; expected 400/403/404. Body (bucket_name/duration_seconds) UNPROVEN. No token captured (safety).
